one_line_takeaway: CoTTA shows that an imperceptible image perturbation (L-inf budget 16), combining a learnable covert text trigger with dual image-to-text and image-to-image feature alignment, can reliably coerce black-box GPT-4o/GPT-5/Gemini-2.5 into emitting exact attacker-specified sentences (up to ~82% ASR), while Claude-4.5 remains largely resistant.
key_finding: CoTTA substantially outperforms all adapted adversarial-attack baselines on GPT and Gemini models. On image captioning (soft criterion) it reaches 81% ASR / 0.442 AvgSim on GPT-4o vs the runner-up FOA-Attack at 47% / 0.232, and averaged over the GPT family it beats FOA-Attack by 31.5% ASR and 0.18 AvgSim. On VQA (hard criterion) it hits 82% ASR / 0.820 AvgSim on GPT-4o and 79% / 0.787 on Gemini-2.5, exceeding FOA-Attack by 46% ASR on GPT-5. Scaled to 1,000 images it beats FOA-Attack by 23.27% ASR / 0.136 AvgSim on average. Ablations on GPT-4o show image-to-image alignment is the single most important component (removing it drops soft ASR from 81% to 27%, a 54-point fall), while the covert trigger contributes ~15 points of soft ASR.
core_contribution: The paper proposes CoTTA (Covert Triggered dual-Target Attack), a targeted black-box adversarial attack that forces closed-source multimodal LLMs to emit an attacker-specified sentence or command while keeping the input-image modification imperceptible (bounded by an L-infinity budget of 16). The authors frame this as a new paradigm they call 'adversarial prompt injection' that combines the stealth of adversarial perturbations with the string-level expressivity of prompt injection. Three claimed novelties are the paper's OWN contribution (not cited work): (1) a covert learnable text trigger imprinted into the image before perturbation, whose scale s and rotation theta are jointly optimized with Adam; (2) a dual-target alignment scheme that simultaneously aligns the perturbed source image to both target-text embeddings and target-image features to overcome the cross-modal modality gap that limits prior text-alignment attacks; and (3) a dynamic target image G_img, initialized from the trigger text mask and iteratively refined by I-FGSM to both pull toward the target text and push away from the current attacked image, improving transferability.
threat_model: Black-box, targeted attack against SOTA commercial MLLMs (GPT-4o, GPT-5, Claude-4.5, Gemini-2.5). The attacker has NO access to victim model parameters, gradients, or internal states; perturbations are optimized on white-box surrogate CLIP encoders (CLIP-B/16, CLIP-B/32, LAION) and transferred. Formally: given clean image x, find x' = x + delta with ||delta||_inf <= epsilon (=16) such that for ALL textual prompts p, the model output semantically contains, implies, or executes a target command c* (prompt-general, command-expressive). Two goal categories are demonstrated: misinformation (VQA, coercing 'None of the above') and action-inducing recommendations (captioning, e.g. 'Search ICML website'). Stealth requirement: the combined trigger + perturbation must remain imperceptible to human observers.
limitations: (1) Claude-4.5 is highly robust to the attack and to all baselines - CoTTA reaches only 8% ASR / 0.046 AvgSim (captioning soft), 7% (captioning hard), and 2% (VQA hard), so the method effectively fails against the most robust evaluated model. (2) Main tables use only 100 images (captioning) and 100 image-question pairs (VQA); a larger 1,000-image run is provided only for GPT-4o/GPT-5/Gemini-2.5 vs a single baseline (FOA-Attack), not full coverage. (3) The attack requires white-box surrogate CLIP/LAION encoders for gradient optimization; success depends on cross-encoder transfer. (4) Baselines were re-adapted to the authors' threat setting (their explicit-text-embedded G0_img used as the adversarial target), which the authors chose - a potential fairness caveat. (5) The paper names GPT-5 and Claude-4.5 as evaluated victims (preprint dated April 2026), so results are tied to specific frontier-model versions. (6) No defense/mitigation is proposed or evaluated; the Impact Statement only calls for future defenses.

datasets_used: NIPS 2017 Adversarial Attacks and Defenses Competition dataset (100 images sampled) for image captioning
ScienceQA (Lu et al., 2022) - 100 image-question pairs for VQA
quantitative_results: Captioning soft criterion, ours: GPT-4o 81% ASR / 0.442 AvgSim; GPT-5 56% / 0.320; Gemini-2.5 79% / 0.429; Claude-4.5 8% / 0.046
Captioning hard criterion, ours: GPT-4o 74% ASR / 0.339; GPT-5 53% / 0.249; Gemini-2.5 81% / 0.348; Claude-4.5 7% / 0.029
VQA hard criterion, ours: GPT-4o 82% ASR / 0.820; GPT-5 58% / 0.576; Gemini-2.5 79% / 0.787; Claude-4.5 2% / 0.020
Runner-up FOA-Attack captioning soft: GPT-4o 47% / 0.232, Gemini-2.5 71% / 0.291; VQA hard GPT-4o 53% / 0.492

Ablation on GPT-4o (soft / hard ASR): w/o covert trigger 66% / 54%; w/o image-to-text alignment 75% / 70%; w/o image-to-image alignment 27% / 22% (largest drop, -54 soft / -52 hard); w/o updating target image 73% / 68%; full CoTTA 81% / 74%
Averaged over GPT family CoTTA beats FOA-Attack by 31.5% ASR and 0.18 AvgSim; exceeds FOA by 46% ASR on GPT-5 (VQA)
Hyperparameters: epsilon=16, 300 optimization epochs, alpha_src=alpha_tgt=1, lambda_pull=1, lambda_push=0.5; LLM-judge success threshold 0.3
baselines_compared: AttackVLM (Zhao et al., 2023) - surrogates B/16, B/32, Laion
AnyAttack (Zhang et al., 2025) - self-supervised noise generator, Ensemble
FOA-Attack (Jia et al., 2025) - Ensemble; strongest baseline
Agent-Attack (Wu et al., 2024) - image-to-text feature matching
